name: AMERICAN SHARED HOSPITAL SERVICES
business: American Shared Hospital Services (ASHS) provides turn-key technology solutions for stereotactic radiosurgery and advanced radiation therapy equipment and services. It delivers radiation therapy through two segments: medical equipment leasing (fee-per-use or revenue sharing contracts) and direct patient services (operating facilities in Rhode Island, Peru, Ecuador, and Mexico). The company's customers include hospitals, medical centers, and individual patients.
namedCustomers: PeaceHealth Sacred Heart Medical Center at RiverBend

exposures.sentence: The Company currently provides Gamma Knife services through its 81% indirect interest in GK Financing, LLC, a California limited liability company ( GKF ) to seven medical centers in eight states in the United States, and owns and operates two Gamma Knife units at stand-alone facilities in Lima, Peru and Guayaquil, Ecuador.

The Company is also the sole owner of PBRT Orlando, LLC ( Orlando ) and the majority owner of Long Beach Equipment, LLC ( LBE ) which were formed to provide proton beam radiation therapy services in Orlando, Florida and Long Beach, California, respectively.
On April 9, 2024, Bristol was granted a Certificate of Need (a CoN ) to provide radiation therapy services in Bristol, Rhode Island.

The Company currently provides Gamma Knife services through its 81% indirect interest in GK Financing, LLC, a California limited liability company ( GKF ) to seven medical centers in eight states in the United States, and owns and operates two Gamma Knife units at stand-alone facilities in Lima, Peru and Guayaquil, Ecuador.

Any failure, interruption, or breach in security of the Company's information technology ( IT ) infrastructure due to a cyber-attack or other security incident could cause the Company to incur financial penalties and losses, reputational damage, and legal liability, which could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business, financial condition, and results of operations.

catalysts.event: First patient treatment at Bristol, Rhode Island LINAC facility
catalysts.sentence: The Company expects to construct a LINAC facility on this real property. The Company anticipates the facility being built and treating its first patient in approximately 18 to 24 months.

catalysts.event: San Javier Gamma Knife facility begins treating patients in Guadalajara, Mexico
catalysts.sentence: The Company does not expect that San Javier will begin treating patients until mid to late 2026.

catalysts.event: First patient treatment at Rhode Island freestanding PBRT system (Johnston, RI)
catalysts.sentence: The Company anticipates the facility being built and treating its first patient in approximately 36 months.

catalysts.event: Extension of Orlando Health PBRT lease through April 2033
catalysts.sentence: The Amendment extends the term of the Proton Beam Radiation Therapy Lease Agreement dated October 18, 2006 between the Company and Orlando Health, as amended by Amendment One to Proton Beam Radiation Therapy Lease Agreement dated effective as of August 12, 2012 (the Lease ) for an additional seven years commencing April 6, 2026 through April 5, 2033 (the Extended Term ).

catalysts.event: Expiration of a Gamma Knife lease contract
catalysts.sentence: The Company expects a third contract to expire in the second quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Fewer than 50 proton beam radiation therapy centers currently operating in the United States
tamClaims.sentence: Proton beam radiation therapy has been available for many years and is recognized as a clinically beneficial alternative to conventional LINACs for certain tumor types. However, conventional radiation therapy remains more widely available and continues to be used as the primary treatment for many cancer types, due in part to the relatively limited number of proton beam radiation therapy centers, with fewer than 50 currently operating in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately eight other proton therapy centers operate or are under development elsewhere in Florida
tamClaims.sentence: There are currently no competing proton therapy facilities located in the immediate Orlando area in which the Company's site operates; however, approximately eight other proton therapy centers currently operate or are under development elsewhere in Florida.

tamClaims.claim: Two other companies actively provide alternative, non-conventional Gamma Knife financing
tamClaims.sentence: The Company estimates that there are two other companies that actively provide alternative, non-conventional Gamma Knife financing to potential customers.
